meaning: n.鑼呭眿,鐢ㄨ寘鑽夌洊灞嬮《
examples: Thatching was less common, but cumbungi (rushes), and blady grass were used if available.
They're thatching a cottage in the village this week.
Thatching is certainly uncommon in United States today.
Traditional skills such as thatching roofs have been lost in the Highlands, according to a council document.
Highland Council has previously warned that traditional skills such as thatching roofs have been lost in the region.
